channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Foote backs squad rotation and continued intensity ahead of Junior Boks clash with Fiji
item.description: Junior Springboks coach Kevin Foote says the team will continue to play with intensity and discipline when they face Fiji in their second match of the U20 International Series in Cape Town. This follo...

item.title: MARINA launches 2-week maritime service contracting program offering ferry fare subsidies nationwide
item.description: The Maritime Industry Authority (MARINA) under the Department of Transportation (DOTr) has officially launched a nationwide Service Contracting Program for the maritime sector aimed at providing fare ...

item.title: WHO reports 220 suspected Ebola deaths as outbreak spreads in DRC and Uganda
item.description: The World Health Organisation (WHO) has reported a serious escalation in the ongoing Ebola outbreak in Central Africa, with its Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus confirming that there have b...

item.title: Toshifumi Suzuki, architect of Japan’s 7-Eleven convenience store empire, dies at 93
item.description: Toshifumi Suzuki, the influential Japanese businessman widely credited with transforming 7-Eleven into a global convenience store powerhouse, has died at the age of 93. He passed away on May 18 from h...

item.title: Privacy-Focused Gay Dating Apps Launch as Alternatives to Grindr Amid User Backlash
item.description: As concerns grow over data privacy, monetization tactics, and bot infiltration on mainstream gay dating platforms like Grindr, several new apps are positioning themselves as community-driven alternati...

item.title: Kenya High Commission collaborates with Australian authorities in investigation into death of Kenyan national Sheila Chebii
item.description: The Kenya High Commission in Canberra has confirmed that it is actively working together with Australian authorities to investigate the death of Kenyan national Sheila Chebii, who passed away in Sydne...

item.title: Prince Harry Reflects on Regrets Over His Early Army and Eton Years, According to Royal Commentators
item.description: A recent Channel 5 documentary, 'Prince Harry In Therapy', has prompted discussion among royal commentators about the Duke of Sussex’s reflections on his past. According to experts featured in the pro...

item.title: Boots Riley Comments on Cannes Film Festival Rejections of His Projects and Selection Choices
item.description: Filmmaker and musician Boots Riley has publicly commented on what he describes as repeated rejections from the Cannes Film Festival. Speaking on social media, Riley claimed that Cannes did not select ...

item.title: Cone commends Abarrientos’ toughness after playing through nose injury in PBA game
item.description: Barangay Ginebra head coach Tim Cone praised RJ Abarrientos for showing resilience and determination after the guard continued to play despite suffering an injured nose during a Philippine Basketball ...

item.title: Aga Khan confirms investment plans in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa during meeting with CM Sohail Afridi
item.description: Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Chief Minister Sohail Afridi met Prince Rahim al-Hussaini Aga Khan V during his visit to Pakistan at Lower Chitral Airport. During the meeting, the two leaders discussed the develop...

item.title: Canada’s leading sustainable toilet paper brand highlights eco-friendly manufacturing strategy
item.description: This article from ROB Magazine, part of The Globe and Mail’s “Made in Canada” series, focuses on a Canadian toilet paper brand positioning itself as one of the country’s most environmentally sustainab...

item.title: Wajackoyah alleges vote rigging in 2022 election and outlines bold 2027 presidential plans
item.description: Former Kenyan presidential candidate and Roots Party leader George Wajackoyah has made fresh allegations regarding the 2022 General Election, claiming that 2.8 million of his votes were allegedly stol...

item.title: Mid-20th Century Christmas Morning Gifts in 1950s–1960s Photos
item.description: The article presents a nostalgic look at Christmas mornings during the mid-20th century, particularly the 1950s and 1960s, using a collection of vintage photographs that capture people with their holi...

item.title: Economic confidence decline raises concerns for Republicans ahead of the midterm elections
item.description: A recent Gallup poll indicating a sharp drop in public confidence about the U.S. economy has sparked concern among Republican lawmakers and political analysts as the country approaches the midterm ele...

item.title: Mayors’ coalition criticizes Senate over prolonged absence of Sen. Bato dela Rosa and salary concerns
item.description: A coalition of Philippine local executives under Mayors for Good Governance has publicly criticized the Senate over the prolonged absence of Senator Ronald “Bato” dela Rosa, raising concerns about acc...

item.title: Zanzibar hosts regional workshop on blue economy for sustainable development
item.description: Zanzibar is currently hosting the 29th Annual Research Workshop, bringing together key stakeholders from Tanzania and international partners to discuss the future of the blue economy and its role in p...

item.title: Haryana CM says youth support for PM Modi remains strong amid ‘Cockroach Janta Party’ controversy
item.description: Haryana Chief Minister Nayab Singh Saini has dismissed suggestions that the so-called ‘Cockroach Janta Party (CJP)’ reflects any real dissatisfaction among young people with Prime Minister Narendra Mo...

item.title: Missing Mossel Bay couple found dead in Kruger National Park; investigation into possible hijacking
item.description: An elderly couple from Mossel Bay, Dina Marais (73) and Ernst Marais (71), were found dead in Kruger National Park after going missing during what was meant to be a holiday trip. The couple entered th...

item.title: Opposition parties criticise Tamil Nadu government over recent crimes and law and order concerns
item.description: Leaders from several opposition parties in Tamil Nadu strongly criticised the ruling TVK government headed by Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay over the recent rise in crimes and alleged deterioration of...

item.title: India tops world in issuing Nagoya Protocol compliance certificates for genetic resource access
item.description: India has emerged as the global leader in issuing Internationally Recognised Certificates of Compliance (IRCCs) under the Nagoya Protocol, which governs access to genetic resources and associated trad...
